description_long: This is a vintage industrial control cabinet, reminiscent of mid-20th-century computing or power management consoles, with a robust, rectangular metal body. The front panel features an array of red and black push buttons, toggle switches, indicator lights, and a central section with a yellow-black striped caution area, likely for emergency or critical operations. A prominent CRT-style monitor is embedded in the upper left, displaying the IBM logo, and is surrounded by various dials, meters, and analog readouts. The surface is finished in a worn, matte blue-gray paint, showing signs of age and use, with some areas appearing slightly scratched or weathered. The sides and back are plain, solid metal, with no visible access panels, handles, or ventilation grilles. This cabinet is designed to be floor-standing and would typically be found in control rooms, substations, or industrial facilities.
description: A vintage, floor-standing industrial control cabinet with a CRT monitor, numerous buttons, switches, and indicator lights on a worn blue-gray metal body.
description_view_0: The front view shows the full control interface, including the CRT monitor, buttons, switches, caution-marked area, and indicator lights.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the angled control panel and the depth of the cabinet, with the buttons and monitor protruding slightly.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a plain, solid metal back with no visible features or access points.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the angled panel and the arrangement of controls from a different perspective.
